method_atom: A high-order numerical scheme for evolving a conserved, non-negative probability density transported by an advection (transport) equation whose velocity field depends self-consistently on the density itself. The density is advanced along characteristic trajectories (Lagrangian dynamics) and re-projected onto a fixed Eulerian grid using a discontinuous-Galerkin discretization, with operator (Strang) splitting plus a higher-order method of characteristics to reach fourth-order temporal accuracy, and correction steps guaranteeing exact conservation of the total integral (mass) and preservation of positivity. The self-consistent driving field is recovered by solving an associated elliptic (Poisson-type) equation via a smallest-stencil local discontinuous-Galerkin method.
object_atom: A collisionless plasma described by the 1+1 Vlasov-Poisson system: a phase-space distribution (probability density over position and velocity) that self-interacts through an electrostatic force generated by its own charge distribution.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the semi-Lagrangian discontinuous-Galerkin machinery for high-order, mass-conserving, positivity-preserving transport of a density under a self-consistent field; the domain-specific part is the collisionless plasma governed by the Vlasov-Poisson equations.
